posts.name: Brian Buzzini
posts.cooked:
Coming off of the older post “Got any Obscure stuff” Got any obscure stuff? - WINE TALK - WineBerserkers name the wines you have that you are the SOLE OWNER of according to Cellar Tracker. Or if you don’t use CT, but think you have the only one.

Me:

1984 Beaulieu Vineyard Pinot Noir Reserve
1993 Domaine Bertheau (Pierre et François) Chambolle-Musigny(3)
1990 Pierre Bourée Fils Côte de Nuits Villages(2)
1990 Pierre Bourée Fils Gevrey-Chambertin 1er Cru Lavaux St. Jacques
2004 Copain Viognier Late Harvest
2001 Dobra Zemlja Viognier
1989 Heitz Cellar Chardonnay Alicia A Nectar of Chardonnay(2)
1966 Heitz Cellar Pinot Blanc
1968 Inglenook Pinot Noir Red Pinot
1998 Novy Family Wines Syrah Sonoma County Magnum
1993 Domaine Ramonet Chassagne-Montrachet 1er Cru Les Vergers
1996 Emmanuel Rouget Bourgogne(2)
1996 Emmanuel Rouget Bourgogne-Aligoté(2)
1991 Round Hill Cabernet Sauvignon The Lodge at Pebble Beach #18 Hole Pebble Beach etched
1989 A. et P. de Villaine Bourgogne Côte Chalonnaise La Digoine
NV Wente Vineyards Pinot Noir Special Selection Bottled 4-23-63
1969 Werner Molitor Kinheimer Hubertuslay Rosenberg Riesling feine Auslese
NV Windsor Vineyards Pinot Noir Special Burgundy Bottling #B206 Release 5/1/71

More than I thought?

link_counts.title: Got any obscure stuff? - Wine Berserkers - international wine social media, online community, and discussion

posts.name: Jay Miller
posts.cooked:
I’m not on Cellartracker but I checked it for some of my rarer bottles and only found one:

1984 Gonzalez Byass Palo Cortado
which surprises me as it was available until very recently at Flatiron. I thought for sure that older vintages would have been rarer but it seems there’s 30 bottles of the 1966 Oloroso out there.

posts.name: Rich Brown
posts.cooked: Did we ever figure out of there’s a way to pull a report like this in CT…or do you have to just manually look up the bottles you figure to be more rare?

posts.name: Mike Evans
posts.cooked:
Here are at least some of mine:

1998 and 1999 Clos Roche Blanche Touraine Cabernet
1997 Clos Roche Blanche Touraine Cuvée Cot
1995 L. Farou Chinon Clos du parc de St.-Louans
1999 Thomas-Labaille Sancerre Chavignol Cuvee Buster Les Monts Damnés
1999 Franck Peillot Roussette du Bugey Altesse de Montagnieu
1995 and 1996 Château de Targe Saumur-Champigny cuvée Ferry
1997 Domaine Filliatreau Saumur-Champigny Cuvée Buster Vieilles Vignes
1996 and 1997 Château du Hureau Saumur Blanc
1998 Domaine des Petits Quarts (Godineau) Anjou Sec

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
B. Buzzini:

1993 Domaine Bertheau (Pierre et François) Chambolle-Musigny(3)

You have the villages, I have the Amoureuses

More seriously, that 1993 Ramonet Vergers should be drinking well. I had the Ruchottes last week and it was superb, though the restaurant alleged that there was appreciable bottle variation. Did you try it lately?

posts.name: Dale Bowers
posts.cooked: Not a wine, but only bottle listed for Hokusetsu Shuzo Daiginko TK4 sake.

posts.name: Brian Buzzini
posts.cooked:
William Kelley:
B. Buzzini:

1993 Domaine Bertheau (Pierre et François) Chambolle-Musigny(3)

You have the villages, I have the Amoureuses

More seriously, that 1993 Ramonet Vergers should be drinking well. I had the Ruchottes last week and it was superb, though the restaurant alleged that there was appreciable bottle variation. Did you try it lately?

93 Amoureuses…lucky! Can’t imagine how good that is as the village CM is actually pretty amazing right now!
Picked up 2 bottles of that 93 Vergers, drank one a couple years ago and it just ok…some nice crushed rock and chalk, decent citrus…just an aged funk that just didn’t blow off. And we had some other stellar whites(04 pycm chevy, 89 Latour monty,09 Tollot Beaut cc, 09 Marcassin) that kicked it to the curb a little. Hoping the last bottle shines.

posts.name: Warren Taranow
posts.cooked:
Rich Brown:

Did we ever figure out of there’s a way to pull a report like this in CT…or do you have to just manually look up the bottles you figure to be more rare?

My question too.

posts.name: Brian Buzzini
posts.cooked:
Warren Taranow:
Rich Brown:

Did we ever figure out of there’s a way to pull a report like this in CT…or do you have to just manually look up the bottles you figure to be more rare?

My question too.

There’s no way to do it other than look at each one you think would qualify.

posts.name: Rob Isaacs
posts.cooked: 2016 J. Brix Pinot Gris Nomine Amoris Kick On Ranch. Still amazed I’m the only one on CT with this wine, consistently one of my favorite wines every year. Seems very young compared to the rest of these lists!

posts.name: David Schliecker
posts.cooked: 2000 Sheldrake Point Cabernet Sauvignon from thw Finger Lakes.

posts.name: Peter Hickner
posts.cooked:
I found a bottle in my cellar that I didn’t recognize, and I thought I’d open tonight. It can’t be getting any better at this point. Azienda Agricola Miotto Lodovico 2004 Merlot. Not only can I not find it on Cellartracker, I can’t find mention at all, as the winery does not list a Merlot among their offerings.

Maybe I should keep it if it is the only one left in the world.

posts.cooked:

I’m still surprised by a couple of them though.

posts.name: Bret Rooks
posts.cooked:
I have a few which no one else currently seems to have in CT. I’m a little surprised at how many, since my cellar is fairly small…

2001 Churchill/Graham Porto Vintage Quinta do Rio
2016 Croma Vera Albariño
2016 Croma Vera Rosé
2015 Croma Vera Cabernet Sauvignon
2015 Domaine des Sanzay Saumur Champigny Les Poyeux
1977 Domaine Thunevin-Calvet Maury Vin Doux Naturel
2017 Le Pépin Chardonnay Vin de France (a $5 TJ’s chard which has been a serviceable sipper/cooking wine in the past)

…and very close:
2001 Hexamer Meddersheimer Rheingrafenberg Riesling Auslese #20 (looks like I have one of two, and Mr. Levine has the other)
NV Terrassous Rivesaltes Réserve Privée Hors d’Age 6 Ans (12 of the 13 ever entered in CT have been mine…there’s one more out there in the community somewhere)

posts.name: Bruce Klen
posts.cooked:
David Schliecker:

2000 Sheldrake Point Cabernet Sauvignon from thw Finger Lakes.

Just curious what you think is the shelf life on the wine.

posts.name: Dennis Atick
posts.cooked:
I don’t have any where I am sole owner, but there are three or less than each of these…

1970 Filippo Sobrero Barolo
1970 Cantina Terre Del Barolo Barolo

posts.name: Chris. Mc Cart
posts.cooked:
An amazing & surprising # of wines with under 10 or 15 bottles total still in cellars. Quite a few with single digit totals.

You can scroll down your inventory on the main CT cellar screen and pending, in cellars, & consumed are shown at a glance.

In terms of only my holdings in CT:
2014 Domaine de la Presidente Côtes du Rhône Villages Cairanne,
NV Jean-Pierre Colas Pinot Noir Ratafia

posts.name: Andrew Demaree
posts.cooked: 1972 Souverain Mountain Zinfandel, Napa Valley

posts.name: Jim Hanlon
posts.cooked:
Andrew Demaree:

1972 Souverain Mountain Zinfandel, Napa Valley

That’s probably still a good wine. Souverain was a quality producer in the early 1970s and some of the old style zins from back then continue to be interesting, like Joseph Swan.

posts.name: David Bueker
posts.cooked:
Tedious task. Best left for a rainy day.

Started with the German Rieslings, and have two right now. Tons more to review.

2001 Hoffman-Simon Piesporter Treppchen Riesling Eiswein
1999 Strub Niersteiner Olberg Riesling Trockenbeerenauslese

posts.name: Leonard Maran
posts.cooked: 1975 Cuvaiso Cabernet Sauvignon ~Philip Togni Selection, Spring Mountain Vineyards.

I have 2:

1975 Emilio Costanti Brunello di Montalcino Riserva 750ml
2005 Masi Amarone Bottega Del Vino Riserva 3L

-af

posts.name: Jim Hanlon
posts.cooked:
Leonard Maran:

1975 Cuvaiso Cabernet Sauvignon ~Philip Togni Selection, Spring Mountain Vineyards.

Leonard, I think it was you who brought a bottle of that wine to an event I attended more than 10 years ago. One of the most memorable Napa wines I’ve ever tasted, perhaps the most memorable.

posts.name: Andrew Demaree
posts.cooked:
Jim Hanlon:
Andrew Demaree:

1972 Souverain Mountain Zinfandel, Napa Valley

That’s probably still a good wine. Souverain was a quality producer in the early 1970s and some of the old style zins from back then continue to be interesting, like Joseph Swan.

I’ve had the 1970 and loved it. But, 1972 was a weak vintage, so I figure it’s just for looking at (‘72 is my birth year).

posts.name: Jim Hanlon
posts.cooked:
Andrew Demaree:
Jim Hanlon:
Andrew Demaree:

1972 Souverain Mountain Zinfandel, Napa Valley

That’s probably still a good wine. Souverain was a quality producer in the early 1970s and some of the old style zins from back then continue to be interesting, like Joseph Swan.

I’ve had the 1970 and loved it. But, 1972 was a weak vintage, so I figure it’s just for looking at (‘72 is my birth year).

Joe Swan made some wonderful Zins in 1972. Have hope!

posts.name: Richard Frankel
posts.cooked:
Well I only found one, I guess I run with the pack more than most of you.

2001 Albert Bichot Richebourg

3 total bottles, 1 consumed, 2 resting peacefully. I opened the one bottle nearly 4 years ago – it was excellent then, WOTN against a lot of stiff competition. I have to open another one soon.
